questions.question:
Which of the following statements about the Ethics Committee in the Lok Sabha are correct?

  1. Initially it was an ad-hoc Committee.
  2. Only a Member of the Lok Sabha can make a complaint relating to unethical conduct of a member of the Lok Sabha.
  3. This Committee cannot take up any matter which is sub-judice.

Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Ethics Committee of Lok Sabha

The Ethics Committee is responsible for maintaining the discipline and decorum of the House. Its evolution and functions are structured to ensure accountability among members.

  • Statement 1 is correct: The Ethics Committee in the Lok Sabha was initially constituted as an ad-hoc committee in 2000. It became a permanent standing committee of the House only in 2015.
  • Statement 2 is incorrect: Any person can make a complaint against a Member of the Lok Sabha. While a member can file a complaint directly, a non-member must submit it through a Member of the Lok Sabha.
  • Statement 3 is correct: The Committee is restricted from taking up matters that are sub-judice (currently under judicial consideration), as this prevents interference with the judicial process.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements are correct in respect of a Money Bill in the Parliament?

  1. Article 109 mentions special procedure in respect of Money Bills.
  2. A Money Bill shall not be introduced in the Council of States.
  3. The Rajya Sabha can either approve the Bill or suggest changes but cannot reject it.
  4. Amendments to a Money Bill suggested by the Rajya Sabha have to be accepted by the Lok Sabha.

Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Money Bills in the Parliament

Money Bills are governed by specific constitutional provisions that establish the supremacy of the Lok Sabha in financial matters.

  • Statement 1 is correct: Article 109 specifically outlines the 'Special procedure in respect of Money Bills'. It defines how these bills travel between the two Houses.
  • Statement 2 is correct: According to Article 109(1), a Money Bill cannot be introduced in the Rajya Sabha (Council of States). It must originate in the Lok Sabha.
  • Statement 3 is correct: The Rajya Sabha has limited powers regarding Money Bills. It can return the bill with recommendations within 14 days, but it cannot reject or amend the bill on its own.
  • Statement 4 is incorrect: The Lok Sabha is not bound to accept the amendments or recommendations suggested by the Rajya Sabha. It can accept or reject all or any of the recommendations.

questions.question:
With reference to Union Budget, consider the following statements:

  1. The Union Finance Minister on behalf of the Prime Minister lays the Annual Financial Statement before both the Houses of Parliament.
  2. At the Union level, no demand for a grant can be made except on the recommendation of the President of India.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Union Budget and Constitutional Provisions

The Union Budget, or the Annual Financial Statement (AFS), is a vital constitutional document managed under Article 112.

  • Statement 1 is incorrect: Under Article 112, it is the President of India who causes to be laid before both Houses of Parliament the Annual Financial Statement. While the Finance Minister delivers the speech and presents the budget, they do so as mandated by the President, not specifically 'on behalf of the Prime Minister' in a formal constitutional sense.
  • Statement 2 is correct: Article 113(3) explicitly states that no demand for a grant shall be made except on the recommendation of the President. This ensures executive control over the initiation of expenditure.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements regarding "Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam":

  1. Provisions will come into effect from the 18th Lok Sabha.
  2. This will be in force for 15 years after becoming an Act.
  3. There are provisions for the reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes Women within the quota reserved for the Scheduled Castes.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.solution:
Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam (128th Amendment Bill)

This landmark legislation seeks to provide 33% reservation for women in the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies.

  • Statement 1 is incorrect: The provisions will not come into effect for the 18th Lok Sabha (2024). The Act specifies that the reservation will take effect after an exercise of delimitation is undertaken based on the relevant figures of the first census taken after the commencement of the Act.
  • Statement 2 is correct: The reservation will remain in force for a period of 15 years from the date of its commencement, though it can be extended by Parliament.
  • Statement 3 is correct: The Act provides for horizontal reservation, meaning women belonging to SCs and STs will have 1/3rd of seats reserved for them within the overall quota reserved for SCs and STs.

questions.question:
With reference to the Parliament of India, consider the following statements:

  1. Prorogation of a House by the President of India does not require the advice of the Council of Ministers.
  2. Prorogation of a House is generally done after the House is adjourned sine die but there is no bar to the President of India prorogating the House which is in session.
  3. Dissolution of the Lok Sabha is done by the President of India who, save in exceptional circumstances, does so on the advice of the Council of Ministers.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Prorogation and Dissolution of Parliament

The President possesses the power to summon, prorogue, and dissolve the Houses, but these powers are usually exercised on aid and advice.

  • Statement 1 is incorrect: The President exercises the power of prorogation under Article 85(2)(a) on the advice of the Council of Ministers. It is not a discretionary power.
  • Statement 2 is correct: While prorogation usually follows an adjournment sine die by the Presiding Officer, there is no constitutional bar preventing the President from prorogating the House while it is in session.
  • Statement 3 is correct: Dissolution applies only to the Lok Sabha. The President normally acts on the advice of the Council of Ministers headed by the PM. Exceptional circumstances include situations where the Ministry has lost the confidence of the House.

questions.question:
With reference to the Indian Parliament, consider the following statements:

  1. A bill pending in the Lok Sabha lapses on its dissolution.
  2. A bill passed by the Lok Sabha and pending in the Rajya Sabha lapses on the dissolution of the Lok Sabha.
  3. A bill in regard to which the President of India notified his/her intention to summon the Houses to a joint sitting lapses on the dissolution of the Lok Sabha.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Lapsing of Bills on Dissolution of Lok Sabha

The dissolution of the Lok Sabha ends the life of the existing House and affects pending legislative business differently based on the status of the bill.

Status of the BillDoes it Lapse?
Bill pending in the Lok Sabha (whether originated there or sent by Rajya Sabha).Yes
Bill passed by Lok Sabha but pending in Rajya Sabha.Yes
Bill pending in Rajya Sabha but NOT passed by Lok Sabha.No
Bill passed by both Houses but pending Presidential Assent.No
Bill returned by President for reconsideration.No
Bill where the President notified a Joint Sitting before dissolution.No

Based on the rules: Statement 1 and 2 are correct, but Statement 3 is incorrect because a joint sitting notification saves the bill from lapsing.

questions.question:
With reference to the Speaker of the Lok Sabha, consider the following statements: While any resolution for the removal of the Speaker of the Lok Sabha is under consideration

  1. He/She shall not preside.
  2. He/She shall not have the right to speak.
  3. He/She shall not be entitled to vote on the resolution in the first instance.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Removal of the Speaker of Lok Sabha

When a resolution for the removal of the Speaker is under consideration, their status within the House changes under Article 96.

  • Statement 1 is correct: The Speaker cannot preside over the sitting of the House while their removal resolution is being debated, though they may be present.
  • Statement 2 is incorrect: The Speaker has the right to speak and take part in the proceedings of the House while the resolution is under consideration.
  • Statement 3 is incorrect: The Speaker is entitled to vote only in the first instance on such a resolution, but not in the case of an equality of votes (casting vote). This is the opposite of their normal voting power.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements

Statement-I: In India, prisons are managed by State Governments with their own rules and regulations for the day-to-day administration of prisons.

Statement-II: In India, prisons are governed by the Prisons Act, 1894 which expressly kept the subject of prisons in the control of Provincial Governments.

Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.option_1: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is the correct explanation for Statement-I
questions.option_2: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is not the correct explanation for Statement-I
questions.option_3: Statement-I is correct but Statement-II is incorrect
questions.option_4: Statement-I is incorrect but Statement-II is correct
questions.solution:
Administration of Prisons in India

Prisons are a state subject, and their management is rooted in colonial-era legislation that remains the primary legal framework.

  • Statement 1 is correct: 'Prisons' is a State subject under Entry 4 of List II (State List) of the Seventh Schedule to the Constitution. State Governments are responsible for prison management and rules.
  • Statement 2 is correct: The Prisons Act of 1894 is the central legislation that historically placed prisons under the control of Provincial (now State) Governments.
  • Relationship: Statement 2 provides the historical and legal basis for Statement 1, making it the correct explanation.

questions.question:
With reference to Finance Bill and Money Bill in the Indian Parliament, consider the following statements:

  1. When the Lok Sabha transmits Finance Bill to the Rajya Sabha, it can amend or reject the Bill.
  2. When the Lok Sabha transmits Money Bill to the Rajya Sabha, it cannot amend or reject the Bill, it can only make recommendations.
  3. In the case of disagreement between the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha, there is no joint sitting for Money Bill, but a joint sitting becomes necessary for Finance Bill.

How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.solution:
Comparison: Finance Bill vs. Money Bill

All Money Bills are Finance Bills, but not all Finance Bills are Money Bills. Their procedures differ significantly.

  • Statement 1 is correct: Finance Bills (of types I and II) are treated as Ordinary Bills in many respects. The Rajya Sabha has the power to amend or reject them.
  • Statement 2 is correct: For a Money Bill (Article 110), the Rajya Sabha has restricted powers; it cannot amend or reject, only recommend.
  • Statement 3 is incorrect: While there is no joint sitting for a Money Bill, a joint sitting is possible for a Finance Bill if there is a deadlock, but it is not 'necessary' (mandatory) unless the deadlock persists and the government chooses to summon one.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

  1. A bill amending the Constitution requires a prior recommendation of the President of India.
  2. When a Constitution Amendment Bill is presented to the President of India, it is obligatory for the President of India to give his/her assent.
  3. A Constitution Amendment Bill must be passed by both the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha by a special majority and there is no provision for joint sitting.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.solution:
Constitutional Amendment Bill (Article 368)

The procedure for amending the Constitution is unique and distinct from ordinary legislative procedures.

  • Statement 1 is incorrect: A Constitution Amendment Bill does not require the prior recommendation of the President. It can be introduced by either a Minister or a private member.
  • Statement 2 is correct: Under the 24th Amendment Act (1971), it is obligatory for the President to give his/her assent to a Constitution Amendment Bill; they cannot withhold assent or return it.
  • Statement 3 is correct: The bill must be passed by each House by a special majority. If there is a disagreement, there is no provision for a joint sitting.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

  1. The Constitution of India classifies the ministers into four ranks viz. Cabinet Minister, Minister of State with Independent Charge, Minister of State and Deputy Minister.
  2. The total number of ministers in the Union Government, including the Prime Minister, shall not exceed 15 percent of the total number of members in the Lok Sabha.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Council of Ministers: Ranks and Size

The structure and size of the Council of Ministers are defined by a mix of parliamentary tradition and constitutional amendments.

  • Statement 1 is incorrect: The Constitution of India does not classify ministers into ranks. This classification (Cabinet, MoS, etc.) is based on parliamentary convention and the Salaries and Allowances of Ministers Act.
  • Statement 2 is correct: The 91st Amendment Act (2003) inserted Article 75(1A), stating that the total number of ministers, including the PM, shall not exceed 15% of the total strength of the Lok Sabha.

questions.question:
Which of the following is/are the exclusive power(s) of Lok Sabha?

  1. To ratify the declaration of Emergency
  2. To pass a motion of no-confidence against the Council of Ministers
  3. To impeach the President of India

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Exclusive Powers of the Lok Sabha

While the Indian Parliament is bicameral, certain powers are reserved exclusively for the lower house to ensure direct democratic accountability.

  • Point 1 (Emergency): Both Houses must ratify a proclamation of Emergency (Article 352). This is not exclusive to the Lok Sabha.
  • Point 2 (No-Confidence Motion): A motion of no-confidence can only be introduced and passed in the Lok Sabha. This is because the Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha (Article 75).
  • Point 3 (Impeachment): The President can be impeached by either House of Parliament (Article 61). Both Houses participate in the process.

questions.question:
With reference to Deputy Speaker of Lok Sabha, consider the following statements:

  1. As per the Rules of Procedure and Conduct of Business in Lok Sabha, the election of Deputy Speaker shall be held on such date as the Speaker may fix.
  2. There is a mandatory provision that the election of a candidate as Deputy Speaker of Lok Sabha shall be from either the principal opposition party or the ruling party.
  3. The Deputy Speaker has the same power as of the Speaker when presiding over the sitting of the House and no appeal lies against his rulings.
  4. The well-established parliamentary practice regarding the appointment of Deputy Speaker is that the motion is moved by the Speaker and duly seconded by the Prime Minister.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.solution:
The Deputy Speaker of Lok Sabha

The Deputy Speaker is a constitutional officer (Article 93) who performs the duties of the Speaker in their absence.

  • Statement 1 is correct: According to the Rules of Procedure, the Speaker fixes the date for the election of the Deputy Speaker.
  • Statement 2 is incorrect: There is no mandatory constitutional provision regarding the party affiliation of the Deputy Speaker. It is a convention (since the 11th Lok Sabha) that the Deputy Speaker usually comes from the opposition.
  • Statement 3 is correct: When presiding, the Deputy Speaker has the same powers as the Speaker. Their rulings are final and cannot be appealed within the House.
  • Statement 4 is incorrect: The Deputy Speaker is elected by the House from among its members. The motion is not necessarily moved by the Speaker.

questions.question:
We adopted parliamentary democracy based on the British model, but how does our model differ from that model?

  1. As regards legislation, the British Parliament is supreme or sovereign but in India, the power of the Parliament to legislate is limited.
  2. In India, matters related to the constitutionality of the Amendment of an Act of Parliament are referred to the Constitution Bench by the Supreme Court.

Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.solution:
British vs. Indian Parliamentary Model

Though India adopted the Westminster model, it introduced checks and balances to suit a republican and federal structure.

  • Statement 1 is correct: The British Parliament is 'Sovereign' (no judicial review of its Acts). In India, Parliament's power is limited by a written Constitution, fundamental rights, and the 'Basic Structure' doctrine.
  • Statement 2 is correct: Constitutional challenges to Acts of Parliament are handled by the Supreme Court. Under Article 145(3), any case involving a substantial question of law as to the interpretation of the Constitution is heard by a Constitution Bench (minimum 5 judges).

questions.question: The Rajya Sabha has equal power with Lok Sabha in
questions.option_1: The matter of creating new All-India services
questions.solution:
Powers of Rajya Sabha vs. Lok Sabha

The Rajya Sabha was designed to be a revising chamber with mostly equal powers, except in specific financial and executive matters.

  • Creating All-India Services: The Rajya Sabha has more power (Article 312).
  • Amending the Constitution: Both Houses have equal power; a bill must be passed by both separately with a special majority.
  • Removal of Government: The Lok Sabha has exclusive power via No-Confidence motions.
  • Cut Motions: These are part of the budget process where the Lok Sabha has exclusive power.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

  1. According to the Constitution of India, a person who is eligible to vote can be made a minister in a state for six months even if he/she is not a member of the legislature of that state.
  2. According to the Representation of People Act 1951, a person convicted of a criminal offense and sentenced to imprisonment for five years is permanently disqualified from contesting an election even after his release from prison.

Which of the above-given statements is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Eligibility for Ministership and Disqualification

Constitutional and legal frameworks define who can hold office and who is barred from contesting elections.

  • Statement 1 is incorrect: While a person can be a minister for 6 months without being a member of the legislature, they must still fulfill the eligibility criteria of being a member. To be a member, one must be at least 25 years old (for Legislative Assembly). A person who is just 'eligible to vote' (18 years old) cannot be a minister.
  • Statement 2 is incorrect: Under Section 8(3) of the RPA 1951, a person convicted and sentenced to 2 years or more is disqualified for the period of imprisonment plus a further 6 years after release. It is not 'permanent'.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

  1. The President of India can summon a session of parliament at such a place as he/she thinks fit.
  2. The Constitution of India provides for three sessions of the parliament in a year but it is not mandatory to conduct all three sessions.
  3. There is no minimum number of days that the parliament is required to meet in a year.

Which of the above-given statements is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Sessions of Parliament

The summoning of Parliament is a constitutional duty of the President, governed by Article 85.

  • Statement 1 is correct: Article 85(1) states the President shall summon each House to meet at such time and place as he thinks fit.
  • Statement 2 is incorrect: The Constitution does not specify three sessions. It only mandates that six months shall not intervene between the last sitting in one session and the date appointed for the first sitting in the next.
  • Statement 3 is correct: There is no minimum number of days prescribed by the Constitution or law for which Parliament must meet.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

  1. The Parliament (Prevention of Disqualification) Act 1959 exempts several posts from disqualification on the grounds of 'Office of Profit'.
  2. The above-mentioned Act was amended five times.
  3. The term 'Office of Profit' is well-defined in the Constitution of India.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Office of Profit and 1959 Act

The 'Office of Profit' concept prevents conflict of interest by barring legislators from holding executive positions unless exempted.

  • Statement 1 is correct: The Parliament (Prevention of Disqualification) Act, 1959, lists several categories of offices that do not disqualify their holders.
  • Statement 2 is correct: This Act has been amended several times (including 1960, 1992, 1993, 2006, etc.) to expand the list of exempted offices.
  • Statement 3 is incorrect: The term 'Office of Profit' is not defined in the Constitution or the RPA 1951. Its meaning has been evolved through various court judgments.

questions.question:
With reference to the Legislative Assembly of a State in India, consider the following statements:

  1. The Governor makes a customary address to Members of the House at the commencement of the first session of the year.
  2. When a State Legislature does not have a rule on a particular matter, it follows the Lok Sabha rule on that matter.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
State Legislative Assembly Procedures

The functioning of State Assemblies mirrors the Union Parliament but allows for local procedural autonomy.

  • Statement 1 is correct: Under Article 176, the Governor must address the Legislative Assembly (and Council, if any) at the commencement of the first session after each general election and at the commencement of the first session of each year.
  • Statement 2 is correct: In the absence of a specific rule on a matter, many State Legislatures follow the practice of the Lok Sabha as a default or guiding precedent.

questions.question: Regarding Money Bill, which of the following statements is not correct?
questions.option_1: A bill shall be deemed to be money bill if it contains only provisions related to imposition, abolition, remission, alteration or regulation of any tax.
questions.option_2: A Money Bill has provisions for the custody of the Consolidated Fund of India or the Contingency Fund of India.
questions.option_3: A Money Bill is concerned with appropriation of money out of the Contingency Fund of India
questions.option_4: A Money Bill deals with the regulation of borrowing of money or giving of any guarantee by the government of India.
questions.solution:
Incorrect Statement about Money Bills

Money Bills are defined strictly under Article 110 of the Constitution.

  • Option 1, 2, and 4 correctly describe the contents of a Money Bill (Taxation, Custody of Consolidated/Contingency Funds, Borrowing).
  • Option 3 is incorrect: A Money Bill is concerned with the appropriation of money out of the Consolidated Fund of India, not the Contingency Fund. The Contingency Fund is at the disposal of the President for unforeseen expenditure.

questions.question: With reference to the Parliament of India, which of the following Parliamentary Committees scrutinizes and reports to the House whether the powers to make regulations, rules, sub-rules, by-laws, etc. conferred by the Constitution or delegated by the Parliament are being properly exercised by the Executive within the scope of such delegation?
questions.solution:
Committee on Subordinate Legislation

Delegated legislation allows the executive to fill in the details of laws passed by Parliament. This committee ensures the executive stays within its bounds.

  • The Committee on Subordinate Legislation examines and reports to the House whether the powers to make rules, regulations, and by-laws delegated by Parliament are being properly exercised within the scope of the delegation.

questions.question:
With reference to the election of the President of India, consider the following statements:

  1. The value of the vote of each MLA varies from state to state.
  2. The value of the vote of MPs of the Lok Sabha is more than the value of the vote of MPs of the Rajya Sabha.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Presidential Election: Vote Values

The President is elected by an electoral college using a system of proportional representation with a single transferable vote.

  • Statement 1 is correct: The value of an MLA's vote is calculated based on the state's population. Formula: Total population of StateTotal number of elected MLAs×1000. Thus, it varies by state.
  • Statement 2 is incorrect: The value of the vote of all MPs (both Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha) is the same. It is calculated by dividing the total value of votes of all MLAs of all states by the total number of elected MPs.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

  1. Speaker of the legislative assembly shall vacate his/her office if he/she ceases to be a member of the assembly.
  2. Whenever the legislative assembly is dissolved the Speaker shall vacate his/her office immediately.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Office of the Speaker: Dissolution and Membership

The Speaker holds a unique position where their term often outlasts the House that elected them.

  • Statement 1 is correct: Like any other member, if the Speaker ceases to be a member of the House (e.g., through resignation or disqualification), they must vacate the office.
  • Statement 2 is incorrect: Under Article 94, when the Lok Sabha is dissolved, the Speaker does not vacate office immediately. They continue in office until immediately before the first meeting of the new Lok Sabha.

questions.question:
With reference to the Parliament of India, consider the following statements:

  1. A private member's bill is a bill presented by a Member of Parliament who is not elected but only nominated by the President of India.
  2. Recently, a private member's bill has been passed in the Parliament of India for the first time in its history.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Private Member's Bills

A Private Member's Bill is any bill introduced by a Member of Parliament who is not a Minister.

  • Statement 1 is incorrect: A Private Member is any MP (elected or nominated) who is not a Minister.
  • Statement 2 is incorrect: Private Member's Bills have been passed in the past. Fourteen such bills have become laws since independence, though the last one was passed in 1970 (The Supreme Court (Enlargement of Criminal Appellate Jurisdiction) Bill).

questions.question:
The Parliament of India exercises control over the functions of the Council of Ministers through

  1. Adjournment motion
  2. Question hour
  3. Supplementary questions

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Parliamentary Control over the Executive

The Parliament uses various procedural devices to ensure the Council of Ministers remains accountable.

  • Adjournment Motion: Used to draw attention to a matter of urgent public importance; it involves an element of censure.
  • Question Hour: The first hour of a sitting where MPs ask questions to Ministers to elicit information.
  • Supplementary Questions: Follow-up questions asked during Question Hour to probe deeper into a Minister's reply.
  • All three are tools of parliamentary oversight.
